title: auth.provision_user
description: Create an org-provisioned login/password user (workspaces-org-program §C1). username is the FULL namespaced login 'org_slug/local'; the account gets auth_type='login', no email anchor by default, and a first-login policy flag. When password is omitted a crypto-strong one is generated and returned ONCE in the result — the caller must never log or store it. Structured failures come back as {"error": "<error key>"} instead of raising.

username.description: Full namespaced login 'org_slug/local' — exactly one '/', both sides in the stock username alphabet.

password.description: Initial password chosen by the provisioning admin. Omitted/null: the server generates a crypto-strong password and returns it once as generated_password.

email.description: Normally null — org-provisioned accounts have no email anchor (spec C1). A non-null value is stored UNVERIFIED.

display_name.description: Display-name hint: mirrored into first_name and forwarded on the user.registered event for downstream consumers (e.g. profiles).

first_login_policy.description: Which first-login flag to raise: password_change_required or mfa_enrollment_required (spec C2).
